title: The Great Steam Deck Crash of 2026: How The New Pricing Slashed Demand by 80%
excerpt: The Great Steam Deck Crash of 2026: How The New Pricing Slashed Demand by 80%
content_html:
Following our recent article estimating the volume of Steam Machines currently sold on the market, I wanted to also go back to another very interesting piece of data and question: has the change of price of the Steam Deck in May 2026 had a big impact on the number of its sales? Not just in value, but in units sold?

Turns out, we should be able to answer the question using a similar methodology and the weekly top sellers charts. This chart lists the ranking of all products according to their sales in total dollars, regardless of the software or hardware aspect.

Throughout 2025, Valve’s Steam Deck maintained a steady presence in the top five positions of Steam’s Global Weekly Top Sellers chart. Week after week, the SteamOS powered handheld managed to stay around rank #4 or #5, hardly impacted by seasonality. If anything, it was propped up by specific times in the year, such as the pre-christmas season.

In early 2026, it suffered some temporary availability issues because of key components preventing its full assembly. It came back at the end of May 2026, with fresh inventory and a fresh (or stinky, depending on your point of view) price as well. On May 27, 2026 Valve announced the pricing adjustment, framed internally as a response to the RAMpocalypse. This was a massive price hike across models, in the +40% range on OLED models, and getting rid of the cheaper LCD version altogether. Valve is not in the charity business anymore. Move away, peasants!

So what effect did it have in the charts? The answer is in this cute little graph below.

While the rank of the Steam Deck in the Top Sellers List seemed stable at first once it was back, it has fallen like a stone from the top 5 to the 14th position for the past 2 weeks. To casual observers, a six-to-eight position slide could seem like a small change. But when you translate Steam’s revenue-weighted ranking back into actual sales, the underlying shift is massive: we estimate that this could represent as much as a 82% decline in weekly shipment volume for the Steam Deck. In unit sales, we are talking about a shift between selling between 11k and 18k units per week in 2025, and only something like 1.4k to 3k per week since July 2026. Just wow!

We are talking about a near-total collapse in physical demand. And that does not bode well for long term prospects for Steam hardware projects as a whole.

If you want to know more about the methodology, keep reading.

Demand and Price Elasticity 101

To isolate the impact of this new pricing, we first need to establish a baseline for the sales in 2025.

The 2025 Baseline

Throughout last year, Steam Deck pricing followed a deliberate tiered architecture:

  • 256GB LCD: $399 (entry point for people on a budget)
  • 512GB OLED: $549 (mainstream volume driver)
  • 1TB OLED: $649 (enthusiast/premium tier)

We’ll drive some assumptions here (that could be incorrect, but we need to make them anyway) - if we assume a mix of roughly 25% LCD, 45% 512GB OLED, and 30% 1TB OLED based on retailer restock patterns and SteamDB historical velocity, we can calculcate a weighted Average Selling Price (ASP) settled around $540 USD. This has been historically a good pricing vs the competition in handheld PCs, typically going for more premium ranges.

The Brutal Awakening in 2026

On May 27, 2026 Valve retired the LCD SKU entirely and repriced remaining models, with one of the shortest press releases ever:

In a few bullet points this is what changed:

  • 512GB OLED: $789 (+44% vs previous price)
  • 1TB OLED: $949 (+46% vs previous price)
  • Certified Refurbished OLEDs: $629 / $759 (limited allocation, higher than prior retail pricing anyway)

With the sub-$400 entry point removed and no mid-tier alternative introduced, the new weighted ASP should have jumped to approximately $835 USD. A +54% overnight increase in average consumer cost. This is slightly higher than the +40% per-unit increase, driven by the complete disappearance of the lower tier model.

Top Sellers Chart and Unit Sales

Steam’s Global Top Weekly Sellers chart ranks by rolling 7-day gross revenue, not unit volume. The distribution follows a steep power-law curve: the top 5 positions capture a much larger share of total spend, while each step downward reflects a progressively much smaller amount of sales. This is not specific to games, by the way. The same pattern is seen in every entertainment industry: the top sellers sell a lot more than the hundreds of others below them combined.

Cross-referencing SteamDB historical brackets, developer post-mortems on SKU sales, and concurrent user correlation models from Q2 2026, we can approximate weekly revenue bands for such positions in the top 20, on average:

Chart Position Estimated Average Weekly Revenue
Rank #4 to #5 $6.0M to $10.0M
Rank #10 to #15 $1.2M to $2.5M

When the Steam Deck slipped from its top-five perch to #14, weekly gross revenue didn’t just dip a little. It shrinked by approximately 72%. This is complete collapse in total sales.

And the worst is that this is compounded by a higher ASP (average sales price) following the massive price hike in May!

Unit shipment is obtained by dividing revenue by average price:

Units Shipped ≈ Weekly Gross Revenue / ASP

Applying our brackets yields the following comparison:

Metric Pre-Adjustment (2025) Post-Adjustment (Mid-2026) Shift
Chart Position #4 to #5 #10 to #15 -8 to -10 ranks
Weekly Gross Revenue $6.0M to $10.0M $1.2M to $2.5M ≈ -72%
Average Selling Price ~$540 ~$835 +54%
Weekly Units Sold 11k to 18k units 1.4k to 3k units ≈ -82%

The effect could not be clearer: a 72% revenue contraction combined with a 54% price increase has completely shut down the demand for the Steam Deck, resulting in 82% fewer units sold on average.

Beyond Pricing

This volume collapse shows a fundamental economic reality. There is price elasticity up to a certain point, and the Steam Deck has tested those waters and demonstrated eloquently that it can sell well at $650, but not so much when trending closer towards $1000.

At $399 ~ $649, it represented great value and while it was more expensive than the Nintendo Switch, it was also more powerful and gave you access to a much larger library of games.

Between $789 and $949, that value proposition is not the same anymore. Purchasing a Steam Deck needs to be weighted against other options now that used to be more expensive, but are now at the same price range. For context, the ASUS ROG Ally X currently ships at comparable pricing with higher peak CPU/GPU throughput, better thermal control, and very good Bazzite support if you want to use something like SteamOS.

Valve hasn’t killed the Steam Deck, but they have certainly damaged its future prospects until three potential events occur:

  • Prices go down to what they were. According to everyone in the industry, there are no signs that this would happen anytime soon. Ouch.
  • They release the Steam Deck 2
  • Competition decides to massively hike pricing as well

Hopefully an eventual Steam Deck 2 won’t cost 2 thousand dollars. As a premium device, if it manages to make some games run that nobody else can, this could become a new market advantage. But so far Valve typically buys components off the shelf, and their key advantage comes from vertical integration from hardware to software. Right now that’s not enough to attrack a lot of buyers at this price. The number of people who are willing to spend close to $1000 on a single device has to be much, much lower than anything around the $500 to $600 mark. This is precisely why historically, console manufacturers have been very careful when setting their launch prices. They know that pricing things wrong could absolutely kill an otherwise excellent machine.

While it’s been a few months since the price has changed, it’s still too early to bury the Steam Deck. We need to keep monitoring the trend until the end of 2026, but so far, it looks like the demand has taken a beating and is lying bleeding on the floor. Stay tuned.

sentences.zh: 继我们最近的文章估算了目前市场上 Steam 机器的销量之后，我还想回到另一个非常有趣的数据和问题：2026 年 5 月 Steam Deck 的价格变化对其销量有很大影响吗？

sentences.en: Not just in value, but in units sold?
sentences.zh: 不仅是价值，还有销售量？

blocks.html_en: Turns out, we should be able to answer the question using a similar methodology and the weekly top sellers charts. This chart lists the ranking of all products according to their sales in total dollars, regardless of the software or hardware aspect.

sentences.zh: 事实证明，我们应该能够使用类似的方法和每周畅销书图表来回答这个问题。

sentences.en: This chart lists the ranking of all products according to their sales in total dollars, regardless of the software or hardware aspect.
sentences.zh: 该图表根据其总销售额列出了所有产品的排名，无论软件或硬件方面如何。

blocks.html_en: Throughout 2025, Valve’s Steam Deck maintained a steady presence in the top five positions of Steam’s Global Weekly Top Sellers chart. Week after week, the SteamOS powered handheld managed to stay around rank #4 or #5, hardly impacted by seasonality. If anything, it was propped up by specific times in the year, such as the pre-christmas season.

sentences.en: Throughout 2025, Valve’s Steam Deck maintained a steady presence in the top five positions of Steam’s Global Weekly Top Sellers chart.
sentences.zh: 2025 年全年，Valve 的 Steam Deck 一直稳居 Steam 全球每周畅销书排行榜前五名。

sentences.en: Week after week, the SteamOS powered handheld managed to stay around rank #4 or #5, hardly impacted by seasonality.
sentences.zh: 一周又一周，这款由 SteamOS 驱动的掌机成功地保持在第四或第五名左右，几乎不受季节性影响。

sentences.en: If anything, it was propped up by specific times in the year, such as the pre-christmas season.
sentences.zh: 如果说有什么不同的话，那就是一年中的特定时间支撑了这一趋势，比如圣诞节前的季节。

sentences.en: In early 2026, it suffered some temporary availability issues because of key components preventing its full assembly.
sentences.zh: 2026 年初，由于关键部件无法完全组装，它遇到了一些临时可用性问题。

sentences.en: It came back at the end of May 2026, with fresh inventory and a fresh (or stinky, depending on your point of view) price as well.
sentences.zh: 它于 2026 年 5 月下旬回归，库存新鲜，价格也新鲜（或臭，取决于你的观点）。

sentences.en: On May 27, 2026 Valve announced the pricing adjustment, framed internally as a response to the RAMpocalypse.
sentences.zh: 2026 年 5 月 27 日，Valve 宣布了价格调整，内部框架是为了应对 RAMpocalypse。

sentences.en: This was a massive price hike across models, in the +40% range on OLED models, and getting rid of the cheaper LCD version altogether.
sentences.zh: 各型号的价格大幅上涨，OLED 型号的价格上涨了 40%，并且完全放弃了更便宜的 LCD 版本。

sentences.en: Valve is not in the charity business anymore.
sentences.zh: Valve 不再涉足慈善事业。

sentences.en: Move away, peasants!
sentences.zh: 农民们，走开吧！

sentences.zh: 虽然Steam Deck在回归后在畅销榜中的排名一开始看起来很稳定，但在过去的两周里它已经从前5名直线下降到了第14名。

sentences.en: To casual observers, a six-to-eight position slide could seem like a small change.
sentences.zh: 对于不经意的观察者来说，六到八个位置的幻灯片似乎是一个很小的变化。

sentences.en: But when you translate Steam’s revenue-weighted ranking back into actual sales, the underlying shift is massive: we estimate that this could represent as much as a 82% decline in weekly shipment volume for the Steam Deck.
sentences.zh: 但当你将 Steam 的收入加权排名转化为实际销售额时，潜在的转变是巨大的：我们估计，这可能意味着 Steam Deck 的每周出货量下降了 82%。

sentences.en: In unit sales, we are talking about a shift between selling between 11k and 18k units per week in 2025, and only something like 1.4k to 3k per week since July 2026.
sentences.zh: 在销量方面，我们讨论的是 2025 年每周销量在 11,000 到 18,000 辆之间的转变，而自 2026 年 7 月以来每周销量仅为 1,400 到 3,000 辆左右。

sentences.en: Just wow!
sentences.zh: 只是哇！

blocks.html_en: We are talking about a near-total collapse in physical demand. And that does not bode well for long term prospects for Steam hardware projects as a whole.

sentences.en: We are talking about a near-total collapse in physical demand.
sentences.zh: 我们正在谈论的是实物需求几乎完全崩溃。

sentences.en: And that does not bode well for long term prospects for Steam hardware projects as a whole.
sentences.zh: 这对于整个 Steam 硬件项目的长期前景来说并不是一个好兆头。

This is not specific to games, by the way. The same pattern is seen in every entertainment industry: the top sellers sell a lot more than the hundreds of others below them combined.

sentences.en: Steam’s Global Top Weekly Sellers chart ranks by rolling 7-day gross revenue, not unit volume.
sentences.zh: Steam 的全球每周热门卖家排行榜按滚动 7 天总收入而非单位销量进行排名。

sentences.en: The distribution follows a steep power-law curve: the top 5 positions capture a much larger share of total spend, while each step downward reflects a progressively much smaller amount of sales.
sentences.zh: 该分布遵循陡峭的幂律曲线：前 5 个位置占据总支出的较大份额，而每下降一步则反映了逐渐减少的销售额。

sentences.en: This is not specific to games, by the way.
sentences.zh: 顺便说一下，这并不是特定于游戏的。

sentences.en: The same pattern is seen in every entertainment industry: the top sellers sell a lot more than the hundreds of others below them combined.
sentences.zh: 每个娱乐行业都存在同样的模式：最畅销的产品的销量远高于其他数百个低于其销量的产品的总和。

blocks.html_en: Cross-referencing SteamDB historical brackets, developer post-mortems on SKU sales, and concurrent user correlation models from Q2 2026, we can approximate weekly revenue bands for such positions in the top 20, on average:

sentences.en: Cross-referencing SteamDB historical brackets, developer post-mortems on SKU sales, and concurrent user correlation models from Q2 2026, we can approximate weekly revenue bands for such positions in the top 20, on average:
sentences.zh: 交叉引用 SteamDB 历史数据、开发者对 SKU 销售情况的事后分析以及 2026 年第 2 季度的并发用户相关模型，我们可以估算出此类位置在前 20 名中的平均每周收入范围：

sentences.en: Chart Position
sentences.zh: 图表位置

sentences.en: Estimated Average Weekly Revenue
sentences.zh: 预计平均每周收入

sentences.en: Rank #4 to #5
sentences.zh: 排名#4 至#5

sentences.en: Rank #10 to #15

blocks.html_en: When the Steam Deck slipped from its top-five perch to #14, weekly gross revenue didn’t just dip a little. It shrinked by approximately 72%. This is complete collapse in total sales.

sentences.en: When the Steam Deck slipped from its top-five perch to #14, weekly gross revenue didn’t just dip a little.
sentences.zh: 当 Steam Deck 从前五名下滑至第 14 名时，每周总收入并没有只是下降一点点。

sentences.en: It shrinked by approximately 72%.
sentences.zh: 它缩小了大约72%。

sentences.en: This is complete collapse in total sales.
sentences.zh: 这是总销售额的彻底崩溃。

blocks.html_en: And the worst is that this is compounded by a higher ASP (average sales price) following the massive price hike in May!

sentences.en: And the worst is that this is compounded by a higher ASP (average sales price) following the massive price hike in May!
sentences.zh: 最糟糕的是，5 月份价格大幅上涨后，ASP（平均销售价格）更高，雪上加霜！

blocks.html_en: Unit shipment is obtained by dividing revenue by average price:

sentences.en: Unit shipment is obtained by dividing revenue by average price:
sentences.zh: 单位出货量由收入除以平均价格得出：

sentences.en: Units Shipped ≈ Weekly Gross Revenue / ASP
sentences.zh: 出货量 ≈ 每周总收入 / ASP

blocks.html_en: Applying our brackets yields the following comparison:

sentences.en: Applying our brackets yields the following comparison:
sentences.zh: 应用我们的括号可以得到以下比较：

sentences.en: Metric
sentences.zh: 公制

sentences.en: Pre-Adjustment (2025)
Post-Adjustment (Mid-2026)
sentences.zh: 调整后（2026年中）

sentences.en: Chart Position
sentences.zh: 图表位置

sentences.en: -8 to -10 ranks
Weekly Gross Revenue
sentences.zh: 每周总收入

sentences.en: Average Selling Price
sentences.zh: 平均售价

sentences.en: Weekly Units Sold
sentences.zh: 每周销售量

sentences.en: 11k to 18k units
sentences.zh: 11k 至 18k 单位

sentences.en: 1.4k to 3k units
sentences.zh: 1.4k 至 3k 单位

blocks.html_en: The effect could not be clearer: a 72% revenue contraction combined with a 54% price increase has completely shut down the demand for the Steam Deck, resulting in 82% fewer units sold on average.

sentences.en: The effect could not be clearer: a 72% revenue contraction combined with a 54% price increase has completely shut down the demand for the Steam Deck, resulting in 82% fewer units sold on average.
sentences.zh: 其影响再明显不过了：收入萎缩 72%，加上价格上涨 54%，完全抑制了对 Steam Deck 的需求，导致平均销量减少 82%。

sentences.en: Beyond Pricing
sentences.zh: 超越定价

blocks.html_en: This volume collapse shows a fundamental economic reality. There is price elasticity up to a certain point, and the Steam Deck has tested those waters and demonstrated eloquently that it can sell well at $650, but not so much when trending closer towards $1000.

sentences.en: This volume collapse shows a fundamental economic reality.
sentences.zh: 成交量的暴跌显示了一个基本的经济现实。

sentences.en: There is price elasticity up to a certain point, and the Steam Deck has tested those waters and demonstrated eloquently that it can sell well at $650, but not so much when trending closer towards $1000.
sentences.zh: 在一定程度上存在价格弹性，Steam Deck 已经测试了这些水域，并雄辩地证明它可以在 650 美元的价格上卖得很好，但当趋势接近 1000 美元时就不那么好了。

blocks.html_en: At $399 ~ $649, it represented great value and while it was more expensive than the Nintendo Switch, it was also more powerful and gave you access to a much larger library of games.

sentences.en: At $399 ~ $649, it represented great value and while it was more expensive than the Nintendo Switch, it was also more powerful and gave you access to a much larger library of games.
sentences.zh: 它的价格为 399 至 649 美元，代表着巨大的价值，虽然它比 Nintendo Switch 更贵，但它也更强大，可以让你访问更大的游戏库。

blocks.html_en: Between $789 and $949, that value proposition is not the same anymore. Purchasing a Steam Deck needs to be weighted against other options now that used to be more expensive, but are now at the same price range. For context, the ASUS ROG Ally X currently ships at comparable pricing with higher peak CPU/GPU throughput, better thermal control, and very good Bazzite support if you want to use something like SteamOS.

sentences.en: Between $789 and $949, that value proposition is not the same anymore.
sentences.zh: 在 789 美元到 949 美元之间，价值主张不再一样了。

sentences.en: Purchasing a Steam Deck needs to be weighted against other options now that used to be more expensive, but are now at the same price range.
sentences.zh: 购买 Steam Deck 需要与其他选项进行权衡，这些选项过去更贵，但现在价格范围相同。

sentences.en: For context, the ASUS ROG Ally X currently ships at comparable pricing with higher peak CPU/GPU throughput, better thermal control, and very good Bazzite support if you want to use something like SteamOS.
sentences.zh: 就上下文而言，华硕 ROG Ally X 目前的售价相当，具有更高的峰值 CPU/GPU 吞吐量、更好的热控制以及非常好的 Bazzite 支持（如果您想使用 SteamOS 之类的东西）。

blocks.html_en: Valve hasn’t killed the Steam Deck, but they have certainly damaged its future prospects until three potential events occur:

sentences.en: Valve hasn’t killed the Steam Deck, but they have certainly damaged its future prospects until three potential events occur:
sentences.zh: Valve 并没有杀死 Steam Deck，但他们肯定损害了 Steam Deck 的未来前景，直到三个潜在事件发生：

blocks.html_en: • Prices go down to what they were. According to everyone in the industry, there are no signs that this would happen anytime soon. Ouch.

sentences.en: Prices go down to what they were.
sentences.zh: 价格下降到原来的水平。

sentences.en: According to everyone in the industry, there are no signs that this would happen anytime soon.
sentences.zh: 业内人士表示，没有迹象表明这种情况会很快发生。

sentences.en: Ouch.
sentences.zh: 哎哟。

sentences.en: They release the Steam Deck 2
sentences.zh: 他们发布了 Steam Deck 2

blocks.html_en: • Competition decides to massively hike pricing as well

sentences.en: Competition decides to massively hike pricing as well
sentences.zh: 竞争也决定大幅提高定价

blocks.html_en: Hopefully an eventual Steam Deck 2 won’t cost 2 thousand dollars. As a premium device, if it manages to make some games run that nobody else can, this could become a new market advantage. But so far Valve typically buys components off the shelf, and their key advantage comes from vertical integration from hardware to software. Right now that’s not enough to attrack a lot of buyers at this price. The number of people who are willing to spend close to $1000 on a single device has to be much, much lower than anything around the $500 to $600 mark. This is precisely why historically, console manufacturers have been very careful when setting their launch prices. They know that pricing things wrong could absolutely kill an otherwise excellent machine.

sentences.en: Hopefully an eventual Steam Deck 2 won’t cost 2 thousand dollars.
sentences.zh: 希望最终的 Steam Deck 2 不会花费 2000 美元。

sentences.en: As a premium device, if it manages to make some games run that nobody else can, this could become a new market advantage.
sentences.zh: 作为一款高端设备，如果它能够运行一些其他人无法运行的游戏，这可能会成为一个新的市场优势。

sentences.en: But so far Valve typically buys components off the shelf, and their key advantage comes from vertical integration from hardware to software.
sentences.zh: 但到目前为止，Valve 通常购买现成的组件，其主要优势来自于从硬件到软件的垂直集成。

sentences.en: Right now that’s not enough to attrack a lot of buyers at this price.
sentences.zh: 目前这个价格还不足以吸引很多买家。

sentences.en: The number of people who are willing to spend close to $1000 on a single device has to be much, much lower than anything around the $500 to $600 mark.
sentences.zh: 愿意在一台设备上花费接近 1000 美元的人数肯定远远低于 500 至 600 美元左右的人数。

sentences.en: This is precisely why historically, console manufacturers have been very careful when setting their launch prices.
sentences.zh: 这正是历史上游戏机制造商在设定发行价格时非常谨慎的原因。

sentences.en: They know that pricing things wrong could absolutely kill an otherwise excellent machine.
sentences.zh: 他们知道定价错误绝对会​​毁掉一台原本很优秀的机器。

blocks.html_en: While it’s been a few months since the price has changed, it’s still too early to bury the Steam Deck. We need to keep monitoring the trend until the end of 2026, but so far, it looks like the demand has taken a beating and is lying bleeding on the floor. Stay tuned.

sentences.en: While it’s been a few months since the price has changed, it’s still too early to bury the Steam Deck.
sentences.zh: 虽然价格变化已经过去了几个月，但现在埋葬 Steam Deck 还为时过早。

sentences.en: We need to keep monitoring the trend until the end of 2026, but so far, it looks like the demand has taken a beating and is lying bleeding on the floor.
sentences.zh: 我们需要持续监控这一趋势，直到 2026 年底，但到目前为止，需求似乎已经受到打击，正在流血。

sentences.en: Stay tuned.
sentences.zh: 敬请关注。

content_html_zh:
继我们最近的文章估算了目前市场上 Steam 机器的销量之后，我还想回到另一个非常有趣的数据和问题：2026 年 5 月 Steam Deck 的价格变化对其销量有很大影响吗？ 不仅是价值，还有销售量？

事实证明，我们应该能够使用类似的方法和每周畅销书图表来回答这个问题。 该图表根据其总销售额列出了所有产品的排名，无论软件或硬件方面如何。

2025 年全年，Valve 的 Steam Deck 一直稳居 Steam 全球每周畅销书排行榜前五名。 一周又一周，这款由 SteamOS 驱动的掌机成功地保持在第四或第五名左右，几乎不受季节性影响。 如果说有什么不同的话，那就是一年中的特定时间支撑了这一趋势，比如圣诞节前的季节。

2026 年初，由于关键部件无法完全组装，它遇到了一些临时可用性问题。 它于 2026 年 5 月下旬回归，库存新鲜，价格也新鲜（或臭，取决于你的观点）。 2026 年 5 月 27 日，Valve 宣布了价格调整，内部框架是为了应对 RAMpocalypse。 各型号的价格大幅上涨，OLED 型号的价格上涨了 40%，并且完全放弃了更便宜的 LCD 版本。 Valve 不再涉足慈善事业。 农民们，走开吧！

那么它在图表中产生了什么影响呢？ 答案就在下面这个可爱的小图表中。

虽然Steam Deck在回归后在畅销榜中的排名一开始看起来很稳定，但在过去的两周里它已经从前5名直线下降到了第14名。 对于不经意的观察者来说，六到八个位置的幻灯片似乎是一个很小的变化。 但当你将 Steam 的收入加权排名转化为实际销售额时，潜在的转变是巨大的：我们估计，这可能意味着 Steam Deck 的每周出货量下降了 82%。 在销量方面，我们讨论的是 2025 年每周销量在 11,000 到 18,000 辆之间的转变，而自 2026 年 7 月以来每周销量仅为 1,400 到 3,000 辆左右。 只是哇！

我们正在谈论的是实物需求几乎完全崩溃。 这对于整个 Steam 硬件项目的长期前景来说并不是一个好兆头。

如果您想了解更多有关方法的信息，请继续阅读。

需求和价格弹性 101

为了隔离这一新定价的影响，我们首先需要建立 2025 年销售的基准。

2025 年基线

去年全年，Steam Deck 的定价遵循了精心设计的分层架构：

  • 256GB LCD：399 美元（预算有限的人的入门点）
  • 512GB OLED：549美元（主流卷驱动）
  • 1TB OLED：649 美元（爱好者/高级）

我们将在这里提出一些假设（这可能不正确，但无论如何我们都需要做出这些假设） - 如果我们根据零售商补货模式和 SteamDB 历史速度假设大约 25% LCD、45% 512GB OLED 和 30% 1TB OLED 的混合，我们可以计算出加权平均销售价格 (ASP) 约为 540 美元。 从历史上看，与手持电脑的竞争相比，这是一个很好的定价，通常适用于更高端的产品。

2026年的残酷觉醒

2026 年 5 月 27 日，Valve 完全淘汰了 LCD SKU，并对其余型号重新定价，发布了有史以来最短的新闻稿之一：

改变的几个要点如下：

  • 512GB OLED：789 美元（比之前的价格上涨 44%）
  • 1TB OLED：949 美元（较之前价格上涨 46%）
  • 经过认证的翻新 OLED：629 美元/759 美元（限量分配，无论如何都高于之前的零售价）

由于取消了低于 400 美元的切入点并且没有引入中间层替代方案，新的加权 ASP 应跃升至约 835 美元。 平均消费者成本一夜之间增加了 54%。 这略高于由于较低层型号完全消失而导致的每单位 40% 的增长。

畅销书排行榜和销量

Steam 的全球每周热门卖家排行榜按滚动 7 天总收入而非单位销量进行排名。 该分布遵循陡峭的幂律曲线：前 5 个位置占据总支出的较大份额，而每下降一步则反映了逐渐减少的销售额。 顺便说一下，这并不是特定于游戏的。 每个娱乐行业都存在同样的模式：最畅销的产品的销量远高于其他数百个低于其销量的产品的总和。

交叉引用 SteamDB 历史数据、开发者对 SKU 销售情况的事后分析以及 2026 年第 2 季度的并发用户相关模型，我们可以估算出此类位置在前 20 名中的平均每周收入范围：

图表位置 预计平均每周收入
排名#4 至#5 $6.0M to $10.0M
排名#10 至#15 $1.2M to $2.5M

当 Steam Deck 从前五名下滑至第 14 名时，每周总收入并没有只是下降一点点。 它缩小了大约72%。 这是总销售额的彻底崩溃。

最糟糕的是，5 月份价格大幅上涨后，ASP（平均销售价格）更高，雪上加霜！

单位出货量由收入除以平均价格得出：

出货量 ≈ 每周总收入 / ASP

应用我们的括号可以得到以下比较：

公制 预调整（2025） 调整后（2026年中） Shift
图表位置 #4 to #5 #10 to #15 -8 至 -10 等级
每周总收入 $6.0M to $10.0M $1.2M to $2.5M ≈ -72%
平均售价 ~$540 ~$835 +54%
每周销售量 11k 至 18k 单位 1.4k 至 3k 单位 ≈ -82%

其影响再明显不过了：收入萎缩 72%，加上价格上涨 54%，完全抑制了对 Steam Deck 的需求，导致平均销量减少 82%。

超越定价

成交量的暴跌显示了一个基本的经济现实。 在一定程度上存在价格弹性，Steam Deck 已经测试了这些水域，并雄辩地证明它可以在 650 美元的价格上卖得很好，但当趋势接近 1000 美元时就不那么好了。

它的价格为 399 至 649 美元，代表着巨大的价值，虽然它比 Nintendo Switch 更贵，但它也更强大，可以让你访问更大的游戏库。

在 789 美元到 949 美元之间，价值主张不再一样了。 购买 Steam Deck 需要与其他选项进行权衡，这些选项过去更贵，但现在价格范围相同。 就上下文而言，华硕 ROG Ally X 目前的售价相当，具有更高的峰值 CPU/GPU 吞吐量、更好的热控制以及非常好的 Bazzite 支持（如果您想使用 SteamOS 之类的东西）。

Valve 并没有杀死 Steam Deck，但他们肯定损害了 Steam Deck 的未来前景，直到三个潜在事件发生：

  • 价格下降到原来的水平。 业内人士表示，没有迹象表明这种情况会很快发生。 哎哟。
  • 他们发布了 Steam Deck 2
  • 竞争也决定大幅提高定价

希望最终的 Steam Deck 2 不会花费 2000 美元。 作为一款高端设备，如果它能够运行一些其他人无法运行的游戏，这可能会成为一个新的市场优势。 但到目前为止，Valve 通常购买现成的组件，其主要优势来自于从硬件到软件的垂直集成。 目前这个价格还不足以吸引很多买家。 愿意在一台设备上花费接近 1000 美元的人数肯定远远低于 500 至 600 美元左右的人数。 这正是历史上游戏机制造商在设定发行价格时非常谨慎的原因。 他们知道定价错误绝对会​​毁掉一台原本很优秀的机器。

虽然价格变化已经过去了几个月，但现在埋葬 Steam Deck 还为时过早。 我们需要持续监控这一趋势，直到 2026 年底，但到目前为止，需求似乎已经受到打击，正在流血。 敬请关注。
